---
title: "Dr. Kadir Jun Ayhan's Website"
pagetitle: "{{< meta website.title >}}"
description-meta: "{{< meta website.description >}}"
main-photo: /profiles/kf_pd_week.png
main-photo-square: /profiles/kf_pd_week.png
title-block-banner: "#004440"
format:
  html:
    quarto-template-params:
      banner-header-class: "home"
---

::: grid
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-5}
```{=html}
<div class="grid" style="--bs-columns: 5; row-gap: 0;">
  <div class="g-col-1 g-col-sm-0"></div>
  <div class="g-col-3 g-col-sm-5">
    <picture>
      <source media="(max-width: 576px)" srcset="{{< meta main-photo-square >}}">
      <source media="(min-width: 576px)" srcset="{{< meta main-photo >}}">
      <img class="img-fluid" src="{{< meta main-photo >}}" alt="{{< meta author-meta >}}">
    </picture>
  </div>
  <div class="g-col-1 g-col-sm-0"></div>
</div>
```
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-7}
Hi. My name is Kadir Jun Ayhan.

I am an Assistant Professor of International Relations at [Ewha Womans University Graduate School of International Studies](http://gsis.ewha.ac.kr/).

My main research interests include public diplomacy, power in world politics, Korean foreign policy, status and reputation, and international student mobility. I serve as the Editor-in-chief of [Journal of Public Diplomacy](https://www.journalofpd.com).

I regularly consult for governmental public diplomacy projects in Korea, including as a member of [Korea Foundation](https://www.kf.or.kr/) Steering Advisory Committee for Empowerment of Cultural Exchanges and Cooperation. I served as a member of the [Korean Presidential (Transition Panel) National Unity Committee](https://en.yna.co.kr/view/AEN20220330011000315) in 2022.

I received my Ph.D. (2016) and M.I.S. (2010) from [Seoul National University in International Studies](https://gsis.snu.ac.kr/) and Bachelor of Commerce (2008) in Economics and International Trade from the [University of Auckland](https://www.auckland.ac.nz/).

I published peer-reviewed articles in numerous journals such as *International Studies Perspectives*, *Korea Observer*, *Asia Pacific Education Review*, *Politics & Policy*, *The Hague Journal of Diplomacy*, and *Place Branding and Public Diplomacy*, among others.
:::
:::
